summary: Added --force-templates to Tools/repo-automation-sync.sh so consumers can deliberately re-baseline template entries to source content. Plumbing: one new argparse argument; one keyword parameter on sync_entries; the existing first-time-only guard changes from `if entry.template and target.exists()` to `if entry.template and target.exists() and not force_templates`. Stale-file handling is intentionally unchanged: consumer-added files in template directories survive --force-templates because the manifest entries set delete_stale: false. Two new smoke tests assert both behaviors: --force-templates overwrites a consumer override of base.md back to source content, AND --force-templates leaves a consumer-added prompts/consumer-only.md alone. Docs updated under Customizing prompt fragments to describe the flag.
scope_observed_from: Tools/repo-automation-sync.sh sync_entries first-time-only guard (added in the prior template-first-time-only-sync slice)
automation/reusable-manifest.json delete_stale: false on template directory entries

behavior_added.effect_when_true: sync_entries bypasses the template-existence skip so source content overwrites consumer customizations of template files (prompts/base.md, prompts/slice.md, prompts/review.md, examples/*, pyrightconfig.json).
behavior_added.what_remains_unchanged: Stale-file removal still respects template-entry behavior (returns [] for template entries unconditionally), so consumer-added files in template directories survive --force-templates. Non-template entries are unchanged either way.
behavior_added.applies_to: --sync, --auto-update, --check (drift detection becomes active on template files when --force-templates is set, useful as a dry run of what --sync --force-templates would change).

tests_added: test_force_templates_overwrites_consumer_override: writes a sentinel into base.md, commits, runs --sync (asserts override preserved), runs --sync --force-templates (asserts override replaced with Owlory source content byte-for-byte).
test_force_templates_preserves_consumer_added_files: writes automation/prompts/consumer-only.md, commits, runs --sync --force-templates, asserts the consumer-added file and content remain.
behavior_proven: --force-templates plumbs from argparse through sync_entries correctly.
Per-file template guard respects the flag: template + target.exists + force_templates = copy; template + target.exists + not force_templates = skip.
Stale-file removal continues to skip template entries regardless of force flag, preserving consumer-added files.
Default --sync behavior unchanged when --force-templates is not passed.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: Workflow templates in manifest (promotes external CI scaffold into Owlory).
Real third-party repo migration when a target is named explicitly by the user.
Sync tool template-update-detection notice (proactively warn when an upstream template file has changed even though local is preserved).
not_claimed: Every template-flagged entry has been smoke-tested under --force-templates. The smoke focuses on prompts/. examples/ and pyrightconfig.json go through the same code path so are presumed safe.
The flag has been exercised under --auto-update or --check modes via tests. Only --sync mode is asserted by smoke tests; the other modes go through the same plumbing.
Real consumer machines have used --force-templates to recover from a deliberate re-baseline. The smoke test bootstraps temp consumers only.
residual_risks: Consumers who run --force-templates without realizing it overwrites local prompt customizations will lose work. This is the explicit purpose of the flag, but the docs name the trade-off so a casual reader doesn't mistake it for a benign refresh.

If a future slice changes stale_files() to remove template-dir extras under --force-templates, consumer-added files would also be wiped. The current behavior is intentional and the test asserts it; that test would catch the regression.
recommended_next_reason: Queue empty. Remaining named boundaries: workflow templates in manifest, real third-party migration, template-update-detection notice.
